title: Surface catalog (the ontology meta-contract)
description: The single schema-governed catalog enumerating one meta-contract record per engine surface. The meta-contract is the record shape every surface entry satisfies (see $defs/surfaceRecord). This catalog carries two laws of the grammar: (1) AMEND-FIRST — a surface is named in this catalog before any instance of it is authored, and rework of a surface amends this catalog first; the grammar precedes the content. (2) COVERAGE — every catalogued surface has its declared `location` home, no orphan surface directory exists, and no uncatalogued surface is in use; coverage staleness fails loud. Enforcement teeth for both arrive with the validation engine (the `coverage` check-kind); at this stage the catalog is committed as authored data and the two laws are documented, not yet machine-enforced. Membership stays sized to the v1 surface set by locking these laws, not the catalog's contents — a new surface attaches additively (an amend-first edit) without changing this schema.

$schema.description: Optional pointer from the catalog data file to this governing schema, for editor and tooling validation.

surfaces.description: One meta-contract record per surface, keyed by the surface's canonical name. The `name` meta-contract field is carried as the map key, so one-record-per-surface is structural (object keys are unique).

surfaceRecord.description: The surface meta-contract: the record every surface declaration satisfies. `name` is carried as the map key; the remaining seven authored fields are below. No other fields are permitted (the grammar does not invent record fields).

class.description: Decides which governance applies. prose = body governed by a template, frontmatter by a schema; structured = whole file governed by a schema; code = governed by tests and lint, carrying neither schema nor template.

location.description: Where instances live, under the repository-topology placement law (one home per surface). Engine surfaces live under .engine/<surface>/; Claude-native surfaces (skill, agent) live where the tool dictates, under .claude/.

purpose.description: One line: what the surface is for.

authority.description: Semantic precedence — which statement governs when two disagree. Precedence: decisions > standing-rules > mechanics-and-guidance > derived-observational. Tiers 1-2 are reserved by law to the self-referential core (`contract` is the sole decisions surface; `policy` the sole standing-rules surface); additive surfaces occupy mechanics-and-guidance. derived-observational is the tier of system-owned outputs (reports, telemetry, knowledge-graph output), which are not catalogued; it is named here only for grammatical completeness.

lifecycle.description: Which lifecycle vocabulary instances follow. decision = proposed -> accepted -> superseded (supersession feeds authority resolution); artifact = active -> deprecated -> retired.

governing_schema.description: Reference to the schema governing the surface's structured content (the whole file for structured surfaces; the frontmatter for prose surfaces). null where none applies: code surfaces are not schema-governed, and a surface whose schema is authored in a later build slice carries null until then, filled by an amend-first catalog edit.

template.description: Reference to the surface's template (the scaffold-plus-shape-spec governing the prose body). Prose surfaces only; null for structured and code surfaces, and null for a prose surface whose template is authored in a later build slice (filled amend-first).
